cards.claim_scope: only default-codec scalar/base-field/affine-point/affine-point-vector behavior on the pinned release, for the stated valid-value, uint32-length, successful-I/O, and allocation-resource domain; oversized-vector rejection, adversarial-stream resource safety, raw mode, other dynamic types, parallel-safety claims, and protocol soundness are excluded

contract.byte_order: big-endian field bytes and big-endian uint32 vector count
contract.canonicality: field decode uses SetBytesCanonical; point decode checks its encoding and subgroup by default
known_nonclaims: encoder rejection of point vectors longer than 2^32-1
memory or denial-of-service safety for adversarial stream counts
types other than the four stated ones

point_decode_paths.single: read compressed-size prefix, extend for raw metadata when necessary, then setBytes with the default subgroup flag
point_decode_paths.vector_compressed: read count and each compressed X, reconstruct Y from the curve equation, then apply the default subgroup flag
point_decode_paths.vector_uncompressed: read canonical X/Y with setBytes, then apply subgroup membership in the vector post-pass

contract.roundtrip: for the stated valid-value and vector-length domain, successful writer/reader operations, and feasible decoder allocation, the executable contract round-trips all four stated types and consumes the complete byte stream

vector_length_domain.decode_resource_condition: the decoder must have sufficient address space and memory for the uint32-counted result
vector_length_domain.enforcement: caller-enforced; encoder performs unchecked uint32(len(t)) narrowing

ok  	github.com/consensys/gnark-crypto/ecc/grumpkin	0.356s

inputs: a valid scalar-field element or base-field element
a valid affine point in the decoder-accepted subgroup (including the encoded infinity)
a vector of such affine points whose length is at most 4294967295 (2^32-1)
cards.mapping: default field branches use canonical big-endian Bytes and SetBytesCanonical; single-point and point-vector branches write compressed point Bytes, preserve slice order, and decode through canonical coordinate, curve-recovery, and default subgroup-check paths; the vector encoder writes uint32(len(t)) without its own overflow rejection, so this binding is defined only when the caller supplies len(t) <= 2^32-1 and decoding allocation is feasible
cards.output: the default release-pinned canonical binary representation within the stated input and resource domain
preconditions: one of the four explicitly executed types
field values are valid field elements and point values are valid curve points in the decoder-accepted subgroup (or the encoded infinity)
for a point vector, 0 <= len(t) <= 4294967295 (2^32-1); the caller enforces this because the encoder does not reject an oversized length before uint32 narrowing
the writer and reader complete successfully and the decoder has sufficient address space and memory to allocate the result
